new_classes.label_en: Distillation process from fermented sugar-cane products
new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous distillation process in which the raw material consists of fermented sugar-cane products (e.g. molasses, cane juice, cane syrup), yielding rum, tafia, or other spirits classified under CN heading 2208 40, provided the resulting product retains its organoleptic characteristics.

new_classes.label_en: Rum / sugar-cane spirit organoleptic characteristics quality
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a spirit distilled from fermented sugar-cane products indicating that the product retains the organoleptic characteristics specific to rum and tafia as described in the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2208, third paragraph, (3).

new_classes.label_en: Rum and other spirits obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products, in containers holding ≤ 2 l (CN 2208 40 51)
new_classes.label_de: Rum und andere Branntweine aus vergorenen Zuckerrohrerzeugnissen, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 l (KN 2208 40 51)
new_classes.definition_en: Spirit obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products (e.g. rum, tafia) that retains its organoleptic characteristics, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less, classified under CN subheading 2208 40 51.

coverage_explanation: The three key discriminating criteria for CN 2208 40 51 are fully captured: (1) the spirit must be obtained by distilling fermented sugar-cane products (FermentedSugarCaneProductsDistillationProcess), (2) the product must retain its organoleptic characteristics as described in the HS Explanatory Note (RumSugarCaneSpiritOrganolepticQuality), and (3) the container must hold ≤ 2 l (SmallContainer). A minor gap (score −0.10) is that the alcoholic strength threshold (< 80% vol, which situates the product under heading 2208 rather than 2207) is not re-stated here, as it is inherited from the parent class SpiritsLiqueursOtherSpiritousBeverages2208 and no suitable existing quality class exactly captures the < 80% vol constraint for spirits (UndenaturedEthylAlcoholLessThan80VolQuality exists but is defined for ethyl alcohol, not distilled spirits more broadly). The sibling distinction from the large-container subheading (2208 40 99) is enforced by the SmallContainer restriction.
